title: schinus terebinthifolia var. rhoifolia
intro: Schinus terebinthifolia var. rhoifolia, also known as the Rauli or Raulí tree, is a variety of the Palo Santo tree native to South America, particularly in Argentina and Chile. This tree is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Schinus terebinthifolia var. rhoifolia include its ability to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate pain. The herbal preparations of this plant include its essential oil, which can be extracted through steam distillation and used in aromatherapy, and its leaves, which can be made into teas or infusions for medicinal purposes.
